text: A Flask web app for Telangana restaurant discovery, UPI checkout, diet recommendations, and admin or staff order operations.

text: PROJECT SCOPE
textPreview: PROJECT SCOPE

text: Problem statement and project goal
textPreview: Problem statement and project goal

text: FoodSprint combines restaurant discovery, ordering, payment, and operations management in one demo product.
textPreview: FoodSprint combines restaurant discovery, ordering, payment, and operations management in one demo product.

text: User need
textPreview: User need

text:
Users need one place to
discover restaurants near
them, browse menus, place
orders, and track status
without switching tools.
textPreview: Users need one place to | discover restaurants near | them, browse menus, place | orders, and track status | without switching tools.

text: Project scope
textPreview: Project scope

text:
The app focuses on Telangana
restaurant discovery, UPI QR
payment, order tracking,
diet-aware suggestions, and
role-based dashboards.
textPreview: The app focuses on Telangana | restaurant discovery, UPI QR | payment, order tracking, | diet-aware suggestions, and | role-based dashboards.

text: Operations view
textPreview: Operations view

text:
Admin and staff flows
strengthen the app by
covering payment setup,
staff access, and order
fulfillment updates.
textPreview: Admin and staff flows | strengthen the app by | covering payment setup, | staff access, and order | fulfillment updates.

text: SYSTEM DESIGN
textPreview: SYSTEM DESIGN

text: Architecture and technology stack
textPreview: Architecture and technology stack

text: FoodSprint uses a compact Flask architecture with clear service boundaries and practical integrations.
textPreview: FoodSprint uses a compact Flask architecture with clear service boundaries and practical integrations.

text: Frontend
textPreview: Frontend

text:
Jinja templates, CSS,
JavaScript, Leaflet maps,
nearby filters, and
mini-cart interactions
create the user-facing
experience.
textPreview: Jinja templates, CSS, | JavaScript, Leaflet maps, | nearby filters, and | mini-cart interactions | create the user-facing | experience.

text: Backend
textPreview: Backend

text:
Flask routes, SQLAlchemy
models, recommendation
services, payment helpers,
and order status workflows
handle business logic.
textPreview: Flask routes, SQLAlchemy | models, recommendation | services, payment helpers, | and order status workflows | handle business logic.

text: Data and integrations
textPreview: Data and integrations

text:
SQLite or PostgreSQL stores
the data layer, while
OpenStreetMap, Overpass, and
UPI QR generation support
discovery and checkout.
textPreview: SQLite or PostgreSQL stores | the data layer, while | OpenStreetMap, Overpass, and | UPI QR generation support | discovery and checkout.

text: Prompt
textPreview: Prompt

text:
Changes start from
natural-language
instructions such as
updating README content or
investigating broken website
images.

textPreview: Inspect and patch

AI reviews routes, services,
templates, and config files
before applying focused
improvements inside the
repository.

this presentation.

text: Example 1: README documentation update
textPreview: Example 1: README documentation update

text: A simple prompt was translated into a repo-aware documentation change that matches the current feature set.
textPreview: A simple prompt was translated into a repo-aware documentation change that matches the current feature set.

text: Prompt used
textPreview: Prompt used

text: I want to update README in GitHub about current features
textPreview: I want to update README in GitHub about current features

text: What this shows
textPreview: What this shows

text:
• AI inspected the existing README before changing it.
• Outdated text was replaced with the features actually present in the codebase.
• The final README became clearer for GitHub visitors and deployment reviewers.

text: Example 2: image quality review
textPreview: Example 2: image quality review

text: A visual issue on the website was traced back to image URL mappings in the service layer.
textPreview: A visual issue on the website was traced back to image URL mappings in the service layer.

text: Prompt used
textPreview: Prompt used

text: Some pictures are not proper in the website
textPreview: Some pictures are not proper in the website

text: What this shows
textPreview: What this shows

text:
• AI can quickly inspect where image sources are assigned in the codebase.
• Broken or weak external image links become easier to identify and replace.
• This improves website quality while keeping the fix targeted and maintainable.

text: NEXT STEPS
textPreview: NEXT STEPS

text: Commands you can run after this
textPreview: Commands you can run after this

text: These commands help you run the app, save your changes, and push the project to GitHub.
textPreview: These commands help you run the app, save your changes, and push the project to GitHub.

text:
# Run locally
pip install -r requirements.txt
python run.py
# Save current work
git add .
git commit -m "Update FoodSprint project files"
# Push to GitHub
git push
# Optional Docker run
docker compose up --build
textPreview: # Run locally | pip install -r requirements.txt | python run.py | # Save current work | git add . | git commit -m "Update FoodSprint project files" | # Push to GitHub | git push |

text: Local run
textPreview: Local run

text: Use pip plus python run.py when you want to demo the app quickly from the project folder.
textPreview: Use pip plus python run.py when you want to demo the app quickly from the project folder.

text: GitHub push
textPreview: GitHub push

text: Commit your final changes first, then push the branch to update the repository.
textPreview: Commit your final changes first, then push the branch to update the repository.

text: Deployment
textPreview: Deployment

text: Deploy only when code or configuration changed. README or PPT-only updates do not require a full redeploy.
textPreview: Deploy only when code or configuration changed. README or PPT-only updates do not require a full redeploy.
